minutes_briefs.title: BC Medicare Protection Amendment takes effect April 1, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: On April 1, 2026, the BC government brought into force section 5 of the Medicare Protection Amendment Act, 2003. This activates new rules under the Medicare Protection Act (the law that governs BC's public health insurance system). The change affects how certain medical services are billed and regulated. If you run a healthcare business in BC, you need to know that these rules are now in effect.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your billing practices to ensure compliance with the new Medicare Protection Act rules now in force.

key_dates.label: BC Medicare Protection Amendment takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 46/2026
Made under: Medicare Protection Amendment Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: March 31, 2026

B.C. Reg. 46/2026, deposited March 31, 2026, under theMEDICARE PROTECTION AMENDMENT ACT, 2003[section 16]. Order in Council 108/2026, approved and ordered March 31, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective April 1, 2026, section 5 of theMedicare Protection Amendment Act, 2003, S.B.C. 2003, c. 95, as it enacts section 18.1 (1) and (2) of theMedicare Protection Act, is brought into force. — J. OSBORNE,Minister of Health; R. PARMAR,Presiding Member of the Executive Council.
